key_insight: ComBench shows that Olympiad-level combinatorics is not a single skill: rigorous proof reasoning and constructive realization can diverge sharply across frontier LLMs.
engineering_relevance: For mathematical AI systems, benchmark design should separately measure proof quality and construction validity instead of relying on one aggregate score.
method: Two tracks: analysis-centric proof arguments and construction-centric explicit realizations
Rubric-guided proof grading plus deterministic construction verification
evidence_signal: The benchmark remains challenging: the strongest model reaches 65.4% overall Avg. and 75.3% overall Best@4, and performance differs notably between proof and construction settings.
watch_item: Results are benchmark-specific and may not transfer directly to broader mathematics tasks, especially where creative existence proofs or explicit constructions are required.
paper_title: ComBench: A Benchmark for Rigorous Proof Reasoning and Constructive Realization in Olympiad-Level Combinatorics
